verses.text: Abraham moves south and stays in Gerar. There he again tells Sarah to say she is his sister — the same fear-driven half-truth he tried in Egypt. Abimelech, the local king, takes her into his house. Old fears repeat themselves.

Abraham moves south and stays in Gerar. There he again tells Sarah to say she is his sister — the same fear-driven half-truth he tried in Egypt. Abimelech, the local king, takes her into his house. Old fears repeat themselves.

That night God comes to Abimelech in a dream: thou art but a dead man for taking the wife. Abimelech protests — he hadn't touched her, and didn't know she was married. Genesis wants us to notice his innocence.

That night God comes to Abimelech in a dream: thou art but a dead man for taking the wife. Abimelech protests — he hadn't touched her, and didn't know she was married. Genesis wants us to notice his innocence.

She said he is my brother, he said she is my sister, Abimelech says. I did this in integrity. God answers: I know — that's why I held you back from touching her. God can stop our sin before we ever know we are about to commit it.

She said he is my brother, he said she is my sister, Abimelech says. I did this in integrity. God answers: I know — that's why I held you back from touching her. God can stop our sin before we ever know we are about to commit it.
Restore the man's wife, says God. For he is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you will live. Even when Abraham fails badly, God still calls him a prophet. Identity is not erased by stumbling.

Abimelech rises early and gathers his servants. They are afraid. He confronts Abraham: what have you done? What did I do to you that you brought this guilt on my kingdom? A pagan king teaches the patriarch what is right.

Abimelech rises early and gathers his servants. They are afraid. He confronts Abraham: what have you done? What did I do to you that you brought this guilt on my kingdom? A pagan king teaches the patriarch what is right.

Abimelech rises early and gathers his servants. They are afraid. He confronts Abraham: what have you done? What did I do to you that you brought this guilt on my kingdom? A pagan king teaches the patriarch what is right.

Abraham's defense is a tangle. I thought there was no fear of God here. And she really is my half-sister, my father's daughter. And we agreed on this when I left my country. The truth has been shaved into a useful half-shape.

Abraham's defense is a tangle. I thought there was no fear of God here. And she really is my half-sister, my father's daughter. And we agreed on this when I left my country. The truth has been shaved into a useful half-shape.

Abimelech gives Abraham livestock and servants, and tells him to settle wherever he likes. To Sarah he gives a thousand pieces of silver as a public vindication — so everyone will know she was not dishonored. The pagan king treats Sarah with more honor than her husband just did.

Abimelech gives Abraham livestock and servants, and tells him to settle wherever he likes. To Sarah he gives a thousand pieces of silver as a public vindication — so everyone will know she was not dishonored. The pagan king treats Sarah with more honor than her husband just did.

Abraham prays, and God heals Abimelech, his wife, and his maidservants. God had closed every womb in Abimelech's house because of Sarah. The promise to Sarah is so protected, even a stranger king's household is shut down to keep her safe.
